column12: DESCRIPTION
Systems based on generative AI (e.g. LLMs, diffusion models).
Systems using classical machine learning models ( Like for e.g.  decision trees, SVMs).
Combination of symbolic AI and ML models.
Shows flow of information within the system.
Standardized system modeling notation.
Models processes and workflows.
Captures hardware/software deployment of AI.
Focuses on interactions over time.
Lists system assets relevant to security.
Captures functional requirements and user interactions.
Hierarchical diagrams modeling possible attacks.
Scenarios describing risks and their consequences.
Predefined list of threats (e.g., adversarial ML threats).
Tool performs complete threat modelling automatically.
Tool exists but requires manual input ( for e.g., annotations, labeling).
Entirely manual process.
Assesses applicability or ease of use.
Performance tested on a real-world case.
Tested on synthetic or small dataset.
Compared to other tools on same dataset/test.
Only conceptual demonstration, no real evaluation.
Method difficult to apply in large-scale AI systems.
Accuracy, precision, or coverage issues.
Limited to certain AI domains/datasets.
Heavy reliance on manual steps.
Not mentioning threats  Like e.g., prompt injection, data leakage, hallucination.
Explicit consideration of threats unique to LLMs.
Proposes a novel threat modelling methodology.
Introduces new supporting artifacts or frameworks.
Tailors existing methods to AI/ML-specific contexts.
Any unique contribution not covered above.
